articles.headline: Government Unveils 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' to Bolster Energy Transition
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approves the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and domestic manufacturing.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key components include incentives for electrolyser manufacturing, developing green hydrogen production hubs, and facilitating demand creation.
• Emphasis on research and development (R&D) in cost-reduction technologies and pilot projects for diverse applications.
• Expected to create significant employment opportunities and attract substantial foreign and domestic investment in the renewable energy sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched to promote green hydrogen production.
Phase 2.0 focuses on scaling production and manufacturing.
Incentives for electrolyser manufacturing are a key part.
Aims for India to be a global hub for green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the economic potential of the mission in terms of job creation, investment, and export opportunities.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine the role of government policies and R&D in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has affirmed the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment emphasizes the balance between an individual's right to privacy and the legitimate interests of the state and businesses in processing data.
• The court clarified provisions related to 'significant data fiduciaries' and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• This ruling provides much-needed legal clarity for technology companies and data processors operating in India.
• Critics' concerns regarding potential government access to data and the independence of the Data Protection Board were addressed in the court's detailed reasoning.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act, 2023's constitutional validity upheld by Supreme Court.
Act balances privacy rights with state/business interests.
Provisions for 'significant data fiduciaries' clarified.
Data Protection Board is the enforcement authority.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on digital governance and individual rights in India.
Analyze the key features of the DPDP Act, 2023, and how it seeks to protect personal data.
Examine the challenges in implementing data protection laws in a diverse and digitally evolving country like India.
Evaluate the role of judicial review in shaping digital policy and safeguarding fundamental rights.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building with a gavel in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution by the Supreme Court in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Digital Governance

articles.headline: India-GCC Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stages
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) are reportedly in their advanced and final stages.
• The proposed FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade, investments, and economic cooperation across various sectors.
• Key areas of discussion include tariff reduction, market access for goods and services, investment protection, and dispute resolution mechanisms.
• A successful FTA would provide Indian businesses enhanced access to the lucrative GCC markets and attract Gulf investments into India.
• The agreement is strategically important for India's energy security and its 'Look West' policy, strengthening ties with a crucial economic bloc.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-GCC FTA negotiations are nearing completion.
FTA aims to boost trade, investment, and economic cooperation.
GCC includes Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE.
Strategic importance for India's energy security and 'Look West' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and strategic implications of an India-GCC FTA for India.
Discuss how the FTA could reshape trade patterns and investment flows between India and the Gulf region.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for Indian industries and service sectors in accessing GCC markets.
Evaluate the broader geopolitical significance of strengthening ties with the GCC bloc amidst evolving global dynamics.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states waving together.
articles.trivia: The GCC was established in 1981, comprising six Arab states of the Persian Gulf, with a primary objective to achieve unity among its members.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Glacier Melt in Indian Himalayas, Posing Water Security Risks
articles.explainer:
• A comprehensive study by the Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology reveals an alarming rate of glacier retreat across the Indian Himalayan Region.
• The report indicates that glaciers are melting at an unprecedented pace, significantly faster than previously estimated, impacting river systems like the Ganga, Yamuna, and Brahmaputra.
• This accelerated melt poses severe long-term risks to water security, agriculture, and hydropower generation for millions dependent on Himalayan rivers.
• Experts attribute the rapid melt primarily to rising global temperatures, localized black carbon deposition, and changing precipitation patterns.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for enhanced climate action, regional cooperation, and adaptive strategies for vulnerable communities.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology conducted the study.
Report highlights accelerated glacier melt in Indian Himalayas.
Impacts major river systems: Ganga, Yamuna, Brahmaputra.
Causes include rising temperatures, black carbon, changing precipitation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impacts of Himalayan glacier melt on India's water resources, economy, and environment.
Analyze the various factors contributing to the accelerated glacier retreat in the Indian Himalayan Region.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including mitigation and adaptation measures, to address the water security challenges posed by glacier melt.
Examine the role of international cooperation and regional partnerships in tackling transboundary environmental issues like Himalayan glacier melt.
articles.imageDescription: Panoramic view of melting glaciers in the Himalayas with visible water runoff.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas contain the third-largest deposit of ice and snow in the world, after the Arctic and Antarctic, earning them the nickname 'The Third Pole'.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Stance Amidst Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The central bank's stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic slowdowns and geopolitical tensions as key risks to domestic growth and inflation outlook.
• Projections for Q3 and Q4 FY25 inflation and GDP growth were largely maintained, with a watchful eye on food price volatility.
• The decision indicates a cautious approach, prioritizing price stability amidst persistent external headwinds and evolving domestic demand conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Global slowdown and geopolitical tensions cited as risks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth in the current global scenario.
Evaluate the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with currency notes in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Landing Test for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD)
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• The test involved releasing the RLV-TD from a helicopter at an altitude, followed by its autonomous descent and precise landing on a simulated runway.
• This experiment validates key technologies for an orbital reusable launch vehicle, which aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches.
• The RLV-TD program is a crucial step towards developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system for future Indian space missions.
• Such reusable technology is vital for India to remain competitive in the global space launch market and enhance its space exploration capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD).
Test involved autonomous landing on a simulated runway.
Aims to reduce space launch costs significantly.
Crucial for developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze the technical challenges involved in developing and deploying reusable launch systems.
Examine how ISRO's RLV-TD program aligns with India's long-term goals in space exploration and commercial space sector.
Evaluate the economic and strategic advantages of reducing space launch costs through reusable technology.
articles.imageDescription: Conceptual image of ISRO's RLV-TD descending towards a runway.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first successful orbital re-entry experiment, the Space Capsule Recovery Experiment (SRE-1), was conducted in 2007.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms for India's Prison Administration
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted its report recommending significant reforms to India's prison system.
• Key recommendations include modernization of prison infrastructure, addressing overcrowding, and improving prisoner welfare and rehabilitation programs.
• The panel emphasized the need for a national policy on prisons and the implementation of the Model Prisons Act, 2023, across all states and UTs.
• Focus on reducing undertrial population through speedy trials and alternative dispute resolution mechanisms.
• The report also highlighted the importance of training correctional staff, integrating technology for prison management, and ensuring human rights compliance.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted report.
Recommendations for prison reforms, including modernization and welfare.
Emphasized implementation of Model Prisons Act, 2023.
Focus on reducing undertrial population and improving staff training.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges plaguing India's prison system and the urgent need for reforms.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee report and their potential impact on prison administration.
Examine the role of the Model Prisons Act, 2023, in bringing about uniformity and modernization in prison management.
Suggest measures to improve the rehabilitation and reintegration of prisoners into society, ensuring their human rights.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized image of a prison cell with bars, symbolizing the need for reform.
articles.trivia: Prisons in India are a 'State subject' under Entry 4 of List II of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution of India.

articles.headline: India Hosts 'Voice of Global South Summit' to Address Climate Finance and Debt Challenges
articles.explainer:
• India convened the second edition of the 'Voice of Global South Summit,' bringing together leaders from over 100 developing countries.
• The summit focused on pressing issues such as climate finance, debt sustainability, food and energy security, and equitable access to technology.
• India reiterated its commitment to amplifying the concerns and priorities of the Global South on international platforms.
• Discussions aimed at building common positions and strategies to advocate for a more inclusive and representative global governance architecture.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing solidarity and collective action to achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) amidst global crises.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India hosted the second 'Voice of Global South Summit'.
Over 100 developing countries participated.
Key focus areas: climate finance, debt, food/energy security.
Aims to amplify Global South's voice in global forums.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South in contemporary international relations.
Analyze the common challenges faced by countries of the Global South, particularly regarding climate change and economic development.
Evaluate the significance of platforms like the 'Voice of Global South Summit' in shaping global governance and policy-making.
Examine the potential for collective action among Global South nations to achieve their development goals and address global inequalities.
articles.imageDescription: Leaders from various developing nations engaged in discussions at a summit.
articles.trivia: The term 'Global South' typically refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies and a history of colonialism.

articles.headline: Government Launches Enhanced National Program for Urban Air Quality Improvement
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched an enhanced phase of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) focusing on 131 non-attainment cities.
• The revised program includes stricter emission norms for industries, promotion of electric vehicles, and improved waste management practices.
• Special emphasis on real-time air quality monitoring networks and public awareness campaigns to foster behavioral change.
• The program aims for a 40% reduction in particulate matter concentration by 2026, building on previous targets.
• Inter-ministerial coordination and state-level implementation strategies are crucial for the success of this ambitious environmental initiative.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Enhanced National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) launched.
Focus on 131 non-attainment cities.
Aims for 40% reduction in particulate matter by 2026.
Includes stricter emission norms and EV promotion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major sources of urban air pollution in India and their adverse impacts on public health and the environment.
Analyze the effectiveness of existing policies and programs, including NCAP, in tackling the air quality crisis.
Suggest innovative and multi-sectoral approaches for achieving significant improvements in urban air quality.
Examine the role of citizen participation and technological interventions in successful air pollution control strategies.
articles.imageDescription: Skyline of an Indian city with a visible layer of smog, highlighting air pollution.
articles.trivia: According to the World Health Organization (WHO), air pollution is the greatest environmental threat to human health globally.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Draft 'Ethical AI Guidelines' for Public Consultation
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has published a comprehensive draft of 'Ethical AI Guidelines' for public feedback, aiming to foster responsible AI development and deployment in India.
• The guidelines emphasize principles of fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, privacy, and inclusivity in AI systems.
• They address critical areas such as data governance, algorithmic bias, human oversight, and the societal impact of AI technologies.
• The initiative seeks to position India as a leader in ethical AI, ensuring that technological advancements benefit all sections of society without exacerbating inequalities.
• These guidelines are crucial for establishing a robust regulatory framework for AI, balancing innovation with societal well-being.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog released draft 'Ethical AI Guidelines'.
Principles include fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, privacy.
Addresses algorithmic bias and human oversight.
Aims for responsible AI development and deployment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges and societal implications of rapidly advancing Artificial Intelligence technologies.
Analyze the key principles outlined in NITI Aayog's draft 'Ethical AI Guidelines' and their importance for responsible AI deployment.
Examine the role of government and regulatory bodies in shaping the future of AI to ensure inclusivity and minimize risks.
Evaluate how India can become a global leader in ethical AI, balancing innovation with the protection of human values.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract image representing artificial intelligence with interconnected nodes and human hand reaching out.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog, formed in 2015, replaced the Planning Commission and serves as the premier policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.

articles.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Review Shows Mixed Progress
articles.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation reveals varied progress across states and educational levels.
• While foundational literacy and numeracy initiatives have gained traction, challenges persist in multidisciplinary education and vocational training integration.
• The report highlights the need for increased state government allocation for education and enhanced teacher training programs.
• Digital education initiatives, accelerated by recent events, have shown promising results but require equitable access and infrastructure development.
• The review calls for a more robust monitoring mechanism and localized strategies to address regional disparities in educational outcomes.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Mid-term review of NEP 2020 implementation conducted.
Mixed progress noted across states and levels.
Challenges in multidisciplinary and vocational integration.
Calls for increased state allocation and teacher training.
mainsPoints: Discuss the core objectives of the National Education Policy 2020 and its potential to transform India's education landscape.
Analyze the key challenges and successes observed during the initial phases of NEP implementation across different educational segments.
Suggest measures to overcome hurdles in achieving universal foundational literacy, vocational integration, and equitable access to digital education.
Examine the role of federalism and state-level initiatives in the effective implementation of a national education policy.
articles.imageDescription: Children studying in a classroom, symbolizing education.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those in 1968 and 1986.

articles.headline: New Cyber Security Framework for Critical Information Infrastructure Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) has released a new, updated cyber security framework for critical infrastructure sectors.
• The framework mandates stringent security audits, real-time threat intelligence sharing, and enhanced incident response protocols for entities in power, finance, and telecommunications.
• It aims to bolster India's cyber resilience against state-sponsored attacks and sophisticated cybercrime syndicates.
• Emphasis on indigenization of cyber security products and services to reduce reliance on foreign vendors.
• Training and capacity building for cyber security professionals across critical sectors are key components of the new policy.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Cyber security basics; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC released new cyber security framework for CII.
Mandates stringent security audits and threat intelligence sharing.
Aims to bolster cyber resilience against state-sponsored attacks.
Emphasis on indigenization of cyber security products.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threats to India's critical information infrastructure and their potential impact on national security and economy.
Analyze the key features and objectives of the new cyber security framework for CII.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cyber security posture across diverse critical sectors in India.
Suggest measures to enhance India's cyber security capabilities, including policy, technology, and human resource development.
articles.imageDescription: Digital representation of interconnected networks and a lock icon, symbolizing cyber security.
articles.trivia: The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) was established in India under Section 70A of the Information Technology Act, 2000.

articles.headline: Government Considers New Reforms to Boost Agricultural Credit Access for Small Farmers
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare is reportedly drafting new policy measures to enhance credit access for small and marginal farmers.
• Proposals include simplifying loan application processes, expanding the reach of institutional credit, and leveraging technology for better financial inclusion.
• Focus on reducing informal debt burdens and promoting diversified agricultural activities through targeted credit schemes.
• The reforms aim to address the persistent issue of low credit penetration for vulnerable farmer segments, especially in remote areas.
• Discussions are ongoing with public and private banks, and rural financial institutions to streamline the delivery of agricultural loans.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Agriculture considering new policies for agricultural credit.
Aims to enhance credit access for small and marginal farmers.
Proposals include simplified loan processes and tech leverage.
Focus on reducing informal debt and promoting diversification.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by small and marginal farmers in accessing institutional credit in India.
Analyze the proposed reforms by the government to improve agricultural credit delivery and financial inclusion for farmers.
Examine the role of technology and different financial institutions in bridging the credit gap in the agricultural sector.
Evaluate the broader implications of improved credit access on farmers' income, agricultural productivity, and rural development.
articles.imageDescription: Farmer working in a field with a tractor, symbolizing agricultural development.
articles.trivia: The Kisan Credit Card (KCC) scheme was introduced in 1998 to provide adequate and timely credit support to farmers for their cultivation needs.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Significant Artifacts from Ancient Trade Route
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a trove of artifacts along an ancient trade route in Western India.
• The findings, including pottery, coins, and remnants of ancient settlements, provide new insights into historical trade networks and cultural exchanges.
• Preliminary analysis suggests links to the Roman Empire and various regional kingdoms, indicating a vibrant commercial past.
• The excavation sites are believed to be part of the ancient 'Dakshinapatha' or southern trade route, connecting various parts of India with overseas markets.
• This discovery enriches understanding of India's economic history and its role in global trade during ancient and medieval periods.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society.
Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered artifacts from an ancient trade route in Western India.
Findings include pottery, coins, and settlement remnants.
Suggests links to Roman Empire and regional kingdoms.
Believed to be part of the ancient 'Dakshinapatha' route.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of ancient trade routes like 'Dakshinapatha' in shaping India's economic, cultural, and political history.
Analyze how archaeological discoveries contribute to our understanding of India's past and its interactions with the wider world.
Examine the challenges and methods involved in archaeological excavations and the preservation of historical artifacts.
Evaluate the role of such discoveries in promoting cultural tourism and national identity.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists working at an excavation site, carefully unearthing ancient pottery.
articles.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: History
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Trade and Economy in Ancient India

articles.headline: National Health Mission Review Highlights Progress in Maternal and Child Health, Challenges in NCDs
articles.explainer:
• A recent review of the National Health Mission (NHM) indicates significant strides in improving maternal and child health indicators across India.
• Immunization coverage has increased, and infant and maternal mortality rates have shown a declining trend in many states.
• However, the report points to persistent challenges in addressing the rising burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) and mental health issues.
• Gaps in healthcare infrastructure, particularly in rural and remote areas, and shortage of specialized medical personnel remain concerns.
• The review calls for a renewed focus on preventive healthcare, early diagnosis of NCDs, and strengthening primary healthcare facilities.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: NHM review shows progress in maternal and child health.
Immunization coverage increased; MMR & IMR declining.
Challenges remain in NCDs and mental health.
Gaps in rural healthcare infrastructure and specialized personnel.
mainsPoints: Discuss the achievements and shortcomings of the National Health Mission in transforming India's public health landscape.
Analyze the dual burden of communicable and non-communicable diseases on India's healthcare system.
Examine the critical role of primary healthcare in achieving universal health coverage and addressing health disparities.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to strengthen healthcare infrastructure, human resources, and preventive care in India.
articles.imageDescription: Healthcare workers attending to patients in a rural health center.
articles.trivia: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by the Government of India, subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

articles.headline: Satellite Imagery Reveals Accelerated Coastal Erosion Along India's Eastern Coast
articles.explainer:
• A new study utilizing advanced satellite imagery indicates a significant increase in coastal erosion rates along India's eastern coastline, particularly in Odisha and West Bengal.
• The analysis highlights the dual impact of natural processes like sea-level rise and extreme weather events, exacerbated by anthropogenic activities such as sand mining and mangrove destruction.
• Coastal erosion poses a direct threat to fishing communities, critical infrastructure, and biodiversity-rich ecosystems like the Sundarbans.
• Researchers advocate for integrated coastal zone management plans, including nature-based solutions and stricter enforcement of environmental regulations.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for climate change adaptation strategies and protection of vulnerable coastal areas.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Study shows accelerated coastal erosion on India's eastern coast.
Particularly in Odisha and West Bengal.
Causes: sea-level rise, extreme weather, sand mining, mangrove destruction.
Threatens communities, infrastructure, and biodiversity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major causes and consequences of coastal erosion along India's coastline, with a focus on the eastern coast.
Analyze the socio-economic and environmental impacts of coastal erosion on vulnerable communities and ecosystems.
Examine the effectiveness of current coastal zone management strategies and suggest integrated approaches for combating erosion.
Evaluate the role of climate change adaptation and nature-based solutions in protecting India's coastal regions.
articles.imageDescription: Eroding coastline with waves crashing against exposed land and damaged structures.
articles.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, making it highly vulnerable to coastal erosion and sea-level rise.

articles.headline: India Crosses 200 GW Renewable Energy Capacity Mark, Targets Ambitious Future Growth
articles.explainer:
• India achieves a significant milestone, surpassing 200 GW of installed renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro).
• This achievement positions India among the top global players in renewable energy and demonstrates its commitment to climate goals.
• Solar and wind energy contribute the major share, driven by supportive government policies and declining technology costs.
• The country aims to further accelerate its renewable energy deployment to meet the ambitious target of 500 GW by 2030.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage, and ensuring a stable supply to match demand fluctuations.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
prelimsPoints: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) crossed 200 GW.
Major share from solar and wind energy.
Target of 500 GW renewable energy by 2030.
Challenges in grid integration and energy storage.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's journey and achievements in scaling up its renewable energy capacity and its implications for climate change mitigation.
Analyze the key drivers and policy interventions that have facilitated the growth of solar and wind energy in the country.
Examine the challenges associated with large-scale renewable energy integration into the national grid and potential solutions.
Evaluate the economic benefits and strategic importance of India's transition to a renewable energy-dominated power sector.
articles.imageDescription: Vast solar panel farm stretching towards the horizon, symbolizing renewable energy.
articles.trivia: India is home to the world's largest renewable energy park, the Khavda Renewable Energy Park in Gujarat, with a planned capacity of 30 GW.

articles.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Global Recognition at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received widespread acclaim at a recent United Nations forum.
• Delegations from several developing nations expressed keen interest in adopting similar interoperable digital platforms for public service delivery and financial inclusion.
• The DPI model is lauded for its ability to provide scalable, secure, and inclusive digital services at low cost, accelerating development goals.
• India reiterated its commitment to sharing its expertise and open-source technologies with other countries, fostering digital cooperation.
• This recognition strengthens India's position as a leader in digital transformation and a proponent of inclusive technology for global development.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model gained UN recognition.
Includes Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
Lauded for scalable, secure, and inclusive digital services.
India committed to sharing expertise and open-source tech globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative impact on public service delivery and financial inclusion.
Analyze why India's DPI model is gaining global recognition and its potential for replication in other developing countries.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with building and maintaining robust digital public infrastructure.
Evaluate India's role as a leader in digital transformation and its contribution to global digital cooperation initiatives.
articles.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and other digital services, flowing into a map of India.
articles.trivia: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

articles.headline: India and Maldives Hold High-Level Strategic Dialogue Amidst Regional Shifts
articles.explainer:
• India and Maldives conducted a high-level strategic dialogue, focusing on strengthening bilateral ties, defense cooperation, and regional security.
• Discussions covered shared maritime security concerns in the Indian Ocean Region and collaborative efforts to counter piracy and illegal fishing.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to maintaining a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing respect for international law.
• The dialogue also touched upon ongoing infrastructure projects in the Maldives supported by India and enhancing people-to-people connect.
• This engagement is crucial for stabilizing relations and ensuring mutual security interests amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Maldives held a high-level strategic dialogue.
Focused on bilateral ties, defense cooperation, regional security.
Discussed maritime security in the Indian Ocean Region.
Reiterated commitment to free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Maldives for India's strategic interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the key areas of cooperation between India and Maldives, particularly in defense and maritime security.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in maintaining stable bilateral relations amidst evolving geopolitical influences in the region.
Evaluate India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy in the context of its engagement with island nations like Maldives.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Maldives side by side, symbolizing diplomatic ties.
articles.trivia: Maldives is an archipelago of 1,192 coral islands grouped into 26 natural atolls in the Indian Ocean.

highlights: Government launches 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' to scale production and establish India as a global hub.
Supreme Court upholds constitutional validity of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, providing clarity on privacy laws.
India-GCC Free Trade Agreement negotiations enter final stages, poised to boost bilateral trade and strategic ties.
New report reveals accelerated glacier melt in Indian Himalayas, raising serious concerns for water security and regional ecosystems.
ISRO successfully conducts critical landing test for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD), marking a step towards cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0', consider the following statements:
1. Its primary aim is to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The mission includes incentives specifically for electrolyser manufacturing.
3. It targets a 50% reduction in particulate matter concentration by 2030.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is correct: Key components include incentives for electrolyser manufacturing. Statement 3 is incorrect: The 40% reduction in particulate matter concentration by 2026 is a target of the National Clean Air Programme (NCAP), not the Green Hydrogen Mission.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is/are NOT explicitly part of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model that recently gained global recognition at a UN forum?
(a) Aadhaar
(b) Unified Payments Interface (UPI)
(c) DigiLocker
(d) National Optical Fibre Network (NOFN)
prelimsMocks.explanation: Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker are core components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model recognized globally. While NOFN (now BharatNet) is a crucial physical infrastructure for digital connectivity, it is not typically grouped as a 'Digital Public Infrastructure' in the same functional sense as Aadhaar (identity), UPI (payments), and DigiLocker (data exchange).

prelimsMocks.question: The 'Dakshinapatha' mentioned in recent archaeological discoveries refers to:
options: (a) An ancient Buddhist monastic complex in Southern India.
(b) A historical trade route connecting various parts of India with overseas markets.
(c) A school of ancient Indian philosophy focused on logic.
(d) A type of ancient Indian maritime vessel used for long-distance voyages.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) A historical trade route connecting various parts of India with overseas markets.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The recent archaeological discoveries along an ancient trade route in Western India are believed to be part of the ancient 'Dakshinapatha' or southern trade route, which connected various parts of India with overseas markets, including links to the Roman Empire.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decisions:
1. The MPC's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
2. The MPC currently targets an inflation rate of 6% with a band of +/- 2%.
3. The 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies a reduction in the benchmark repo rate.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: This is the mandated primary objective of the MPC. Statement 2 is incorrect: The MPC targets an inflation rate of 4% with a band of +/- 2%. Statement 3 is incorrect: 'Withdrawal of accommodation' means reducing the liquidity in the system, implying either maintaining or raising interest rates, not reducing them.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is responsible for releasing the 'Ethical AI Guidelines' for public consultation in India?
options: (a) Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
(d) Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
prelimsMocks.explanation: NITI Aayog has published a comprehensive draft of 'Ethical AI Guidelines' for public feedback, aiming to foster responsible AI development and deployment in India.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambitious 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' is poised to be a game-changer for its energy security and climate commitments. Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India and analyze the key challenges in scaling up its production and adoption.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation; Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Green Hydrogen and the mission's goals. Discuss strategic importance (energy security, decarbonization, economic growth). Analyze challenges (cost, infrastructure, technology, demand creation). Conclude with way forward.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's decision to uphold the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, marks a significant step in India's digital governance journey. Critically analyze the key provisions of the DPDP Act and its implications for individual privacy and the digital economy.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government Policies and Interventions; Indian Constitution—significant provisions; Functions of Judiciary.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the significance of the SC ruling. Explain key provisions of DPDP Act (definitions, data principal/fiduciary rights/obligations, Data Protection Board, penalties). Discuss implications (right to privacy, business compliance, government access, digital economy growth). Conclude with balancing innovation and rights.

mainsMocks.question: India's 'Voice of Global South Summit' underscores its commitment to amplifying the concerns of developing nations. Examine India's leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South and discuss the common challenges faced by these countries, particularly regarding climate finance and debt sustainability.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India; Important International institutions, agencies and fora.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the summit and India's role. Elaborate on India's leadership (historical, current initiatives, multilateral diplomacy). Discuss common challenges of Global South (climate vulnerability, inadequate climate finance, debt burden, tech gap). Conclude with need for collective action and equitable global governance.

quickBytes.text: India's renewable energy capacity (excluding large hydro) now exceeds 200 GW, a key step towards 2030 targets.
The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was upheld by the Supreme Court, affirming India's data privacy framework.
The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) consists of Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE.
Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology is a premier autonomous research organization under DST.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to reduce India's reliance on fossil fuels and promote green energy.
Prisons are a 'State Subject' under the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution.
NITI Aayog acts as the premier policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
The National Clean Air Programme (NCAP) targets a 40% reduction in particulate matter concentration by 2026.
The 'Dakshinapatha' was an ancient Indian trade route, signifying historical economic linkages.
India's UPI processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023, showcasing digital payment adoption.

GS1.headline: New Report Highlights Accelerated Glacier Melt in Indian Himalayas, Posing Water Security Risks
GS1.explainer:
• A comprehensive study by the Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology reveals an alarming rate of glacier retreat across the Indian Himalayan Region.
• The report indicates that glaciers are melting at an unprecedented pace, significantly faster than previously estimated, impacting river systems like the Ganga, Yamuna, and Brahmaputra.
• This accelerated melt poses severe long-term risks to water security, agriculture, and hydropower generation for millions dependent on Himalayan rivers.
• Experts attribute the rapid melt primarily to rising global temperatures, localized black carbon deposition, and changing precipitation patterns.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for enhanced climate action, regional cooperation, and adaptive strategies for vulnerable communities.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Wadia Institute of Himalayan Geology conducted the study.
Report highlights accelerated glacier melt in Indian Himalayas.
Impacts major river systems: Ganga, Yamuna, Brahmaputra.
Causes include rising temperatures, black carbon, changing precipitation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted impacts of Himalayan glacier melt on India's water resources, economy, and environment.
Analyze the various factors contributing to the accelerated glacier retreat in the Indian Himalayan Region.
Suggest comprehensive strategies, including mitigation and adaptation measures, to address the water security challenges posed by glacier melt.
Examine the role of international cooperation and regional partnerships in tackling transboundary environmental issues like Himalayan glacier melt.
GS1.imageDescription: Panoramic view of melting glaciers in the Himalayas with visible water runoff.
GS1.trivia: The Himalayas contain the third-largest deposit of ice and snow in the world, after the Arctic and Antarctic, earning them the nickname 'The Third Pole'.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Uncovers Significant Artifacts from Ancient Trade Route
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a trove of artifacts along an ancient trade route in Western India.
• The findings, including pottery, coins, and remnants of ancient settlements, provide new insights into historical trade networks and cultural exchanges.
• Preliminary analysis suggests links to the Roman Empire and various regional kingdoms, indicating a vibrant commercial past.
• The excavation sites are believed to be part of the ancient 'Dakshinapatha' or southern trade route, connecting various parts of India with overseas markets.
• This discovery enriches understanding of India's economic history and its role in global trade during ancient and medieval periods.
gsSubTopics: Indian Heritage and Culture, History and Geography of the World and Society.
Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: ASI discovered artifacts from an ancient trade route in Western India.
Findings include pottery, coins, and settlement remnants.
Suggests links to Roman Empire and regional kingdoms.
Believed to be part of the ancient 'Dakshinapatha' route.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of ancient trade routes like 'Dakshinapatha' in shaping India's economic, cultural, and political history.
Analyze how archaeological discoveries contribute to our understanding of India's past and its interactions with the wider world.
Examine the challenges and methods involved in archaeological excavations and the preservation of historical artifacts.
Evaluate the role of such discoveries in promoting cultural tourism and national identity.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists working at an excavation site, carefully unearthing ancient pottery.
GS1.trivia: The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) was founded in 1861 by Alexander Cunningham, who also became its first Director-General.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: History
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Trade and Economy in Ancient India

GS1.headline: Satellite Imagery Reveals Accelerated Coastal Erosion Along India's Eastern Coast
GS1.explainer:
• A new study utilizing advanced satellite imagery indicates a significant increase in coastal erosion rates along India's eastern coastline, particularly in Odisha and West Bengal.
• The analysis highlights the dual impact of natural processes like sea-level rise and extreme weather events, exacerbated by anthropogenic activities such as sand mining and mangrove destruction.
• Coastal erosion poses a direct threat to fishing communities, critical infrastructure, and biodiversity-rich ecosystems like the Sundarbans.
• Researchers advocate for integrated coastal zone management plans, including nature-based solutions and stricter enforcement of environmental regulations.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for climate change adaptation strategies and protection of vulnerable coastal areas.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including water-bodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Study shows accelerated coastal erosion on India's eastern coast.
Particularly in Odisha and West Bengal.
Causes: sea-level rise, extreme weather, sand mining, mangrove destruction.
Threatens communities, infrastructure, and biodiversity.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major causes and consequences of coastal erosion along India's coastline, with a focus on the eastern coast.
Analyze the socio-economic and environmental impacts of coastal erosion on vulnerable communities and ecosystems.
Examine the effectiveness of current coastal zone management strategies and suggest integrated approaches for combating erosion.
Evaluate the role of climate change adaptation and nature-based solutions in protecting India's coastal regions.
GS1.imageDescription: Eroding coastline with waves crashing against exposed land and damaged structures.
GS1.trivia: India has a coastline of over 7,500 km, making it highly vulnerable to coastal erosion and sea-level rise.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has affirmed the constitutional validity of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The judgment emphasizes the balance between an individual's right to privacy and the legitimate interests of the state and businesses in processing data.
• The court clarified provisions related to 'significant data fiduciaries' and the powers of the Data Protection Board.
• This ruling provides much-needed legal clarity for technology companies and data processors operating in India.
• Critics' concerns regarding potential government access to data and the independence of the Data Protection Board were addressed in the court's detailed reasoning.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act, 2023's constitutional validity upheld by Supreme Court.
Act balances privacy rights with state/business interests.
Provisions for 'significant data fiduciaries' clarified.
Data Protection Board is the enforcement authority.
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the Supreme Court's verdict on digital governance and individual rights in India.
Analyze the key features of the DPDP Act, 2023, and how it seeks to protect personal data.
Examine the challenges in implementing data protection laws in a diverse and digitally evolving country like India.
Evaluate the role of judicial review in shaping digital policy and safeguarding fundamental rights.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building with a gavel in the foreground.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to Privacy' was declared a fundamental right under Article 21 of the Indian Constitution by the Supreme Court in the landmark Puttaswamy judgment (2017).

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Polity
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Right to Privacy and Digital Governance

GS2.headline: India-GCC Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Enter Final Stages
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for a comprehensive Free Trade Agreement (FTA) between India and the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) are reportedly in their advanced and final stages.
• The proposed FTA aims to significantly boost bilateral trade, investments, and economic cooperation across various sectors.
• Key areas of discussion include tariff reduction, market access for goods and services, investment protection, and dispute resolution mechanisms.
• A successful FTA would provide Indian businesses enhanced access to the lucrative GCC markets and attract Gulf investments into India.
• The agreement is strategically important for India's energy security and its 'Look West' policy, strengthening ties with a crucial economic bloc.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-GCC FTA negotiations are nearing completion.
FTA aims to boost trade, investment, and economic cooperation.
GCC includes Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE.
Strategic importance for India's energy security and 'Look West' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential economic benefits and strategic implications of an India-GCC FTA for India.
Discuss how the FTA could reshape trade patterns and investment flows between India and the Gulf region.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for Indian industries and service sectors in accessing GCC markets.
Evaluate the broader geopolitical significance of strengthening ties with the GCC bloc amidst evolving global dynamics.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and GCC member states waving together.
GS2.trivia: The GCC was established in 1981, comprising six Arab states of the Persian Gulf, with a primary objective to achieve unity among its members.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms for India's Prison Administration
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted its report recommending significant reforms to India's prison system.
• Key recommendations include modernization of prison infrastructure, addressing overcrowding, and improving prisoner welfare and rehabilitation programs.
• The panel emphasized the need for a national policy on prisons and the implementation of the Model Prisons Act, 2023, across all states and UTs.
• Focus on reducing undertrial population through speedy trials and alternative dispute resolution mechanisms.
• The report also highlighted the importance of training correctional staff, integrating technology for prison management, and ensuring human rights compliance.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted report.
Recommendations for prison reforms, including modernization and welfare.
Emphasized implementation of Model Prisons Act, 2023.
Focus on reducing undertrial population and improving staff training.
mainsPoints: Discuss the major challenges plaguing India's prison system and the urgent need for reforms.
Analyze the key recommendations of the Parliamentary Committee report and their potential impact on prison administration.
Examine the role of the Model Prisons Act, 2023, in bringing about uniformity and modernization in prison management.
Suggest measures to improve the rehabilitation and reintegration of prisoners into society, ensuring their human rights.
GS2.imageDescription: Stylized image of a prison cell with bars, symbolizing the need for reform.
GS2.trivia: Prisons in India are a 'State subject' under Entry 4 of List II of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution of India.

GS2.headline: India Hosts 'Voice of Global South Summit' to Address Climate Finance and Debt Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• India convened the second edition of the 'Voice of Global South Summit,' bringing together leaders from over 100 developing countries.
• The summit focused on pressing issues such as climate finance, debt sustainability, food and energy security, and equitable access to technology.
• India reiterated its commitment to amplifying the concerns and priorities of the Global South on international platforms.
• Discussions aimed at building common positions and strategies to advocate for a more inclusive and representative global governance architecture.
• The summit concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing solidarity and collective action to achieve Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) amidst global crises.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
prelimsPoints: India hosted the second 'Voice of Global South Summit'.
Over 100 developing countries participated.
Key focus areas: climate finance, debt, food/energy security.
Aims to amplify Global South's voice in global forums.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership role in representing the interests of the Global South in contemporary international relations.
Analyze the common challenges faced by countries of the Global South, particularly regarding climate change and economic development.
Evaluate the significance of platforms like the 'Voice of Global South Summit' in shaping global governance and policy-making.
Examine the potential for collective action among Global South nations to achieve their development goals and address global inequalities.
GS2.imageDescription: Leaders from various developing nations engaged in discussions at a summit.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Global South' typically refers to countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America, often characterized by developing economies and a history of colonialism.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy (NEP) Implementation Review Shows Mixed Progress
GS2.explainer:
• A mid-term review of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation reveals varied progress across states and educational levels.
• While foundational literacy and numeracy initiatives have gained traction, challenges persist in multidisciplinary education and vocational training integration.
• The report highlights the need for increased state government allocation for education and enhanced teacher training programs.
• Digital education initiatives, accelerated by recent events, have shown promising results but require equitable access and infrastructure development.
• The review calls for a more robust monitoring mechanism and localized strategies to address regional disparities in educational outcomes.
gsSubTopics: Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Mid-term review of NEP 2020 implementation conducted.
Mixed progress noted across states and levels.
Challenges in multidisciplinary and vocational integration.
Calls for increased state allocation and teacher training.
mainsPoints: Discuss the core objectives of the National Education Policy 2020 and its potential to transform India's education landscape.
Analyze the key challenges and successes observed during the initial phases of NEP implementation across different educational segments.
Suggest measures to overcome hurdles in achieving universal foundational literacy, vocational integration, and equitable access to digital education.
Examine the role of federalism and state-level initiatives in the effective implementation of a national education policy.
GS2.imageDescription: Children studying in a classroom, symbolizing education.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, following those in 1968 and 1986.

GS2.headline: National Health Mission Review Highlights Progress in Maternal and Child Health, Challenges in NCDs
GS2.explainer:
• A recent review of the National Health Mission (NHM) indicates significant strides in improving maternal and child health indicators across India.
• Immunization coverage has increased, and infant and maternal mortality rates have shown a declining trend in many states.
• However, the report points to persistent challenges in addressing the rising burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) and mental health issues.
• Gaps in healthcare infrastructure, particularly in rural and remote areas, and shortage of specialized medical personnel remain concerns.
• The review calls for a renewed focus on preventive healthcare, early diagnosis of NCDs, and strengthening primary healthcare facilities.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: NHM review shows progress in maternal and child health.
Immunization coverage increased; MMR & IMR declining.
Challenges remain in NCDs and mental health.
Gaps in rural healthcare infrastructure and specialized personnel.
mainsPoints: Discuss the achievements and shortcomings of the National Health Mission in transforming India's public health landscape.
Analyze the dual burden of communicable and non-communicable diseases on India's healthcare system.
Examine the critical role of primary healthcare in achieving universal health coverage and addressing health disparities.
Suggest comprehensive strategies to strengthen healthcare infrastructure, human resources, and preventive care in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Healthcare workers attending to patients in a rural health center.
GS2.trivia: The National Health Mission (NHM) was launched in 2013 by the Government of India, subsuming the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM) and the National Urban Health Mission (NUHM).

GS2.headline: India's Digital Public Infrastructure Model Gains Global Recognition at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India's pioneering Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, received widespread acclaim at a recent United Nations forum.
• Delegations from several developing nations expressed keen interest in adopting similar interoperable digital platforms for public service delivery and financial inclusion.
• The DPI model is lauded for its ability to provide scalable, secure, and inclusive digital services at low cost, accelerating development goals.
• India reiterated its commitment to sharing its expertise and open-source technologies with other countries, fostering digital cooperation.
• This recognition strengthens India's position as a leader in digital transformation and a proponent of inclusive technology for global development.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Government Policies and Interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model gained UN recognition.
Includes Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker.
Lauded for scalable, secure, and inclusive digital services.
India committed to sharing expertise and open-source tech globally.
mainsPoints: Discuss the components of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and its transformative impact on public service delivery and financial inclusion.
Analyze why India's DPI model is gaining global recognition and its potential for replication in other developing countries.
Examine the challenges and opportunities associated with building and maintaining robust digital public infrastructure.
Evaluate India's role as a leader in digital transformation and its contribution to global digital cooperation initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: Interconnected digital icons representing Aadhaar, UPI, and other digital services, flowing into a map of India.
GS2.trivia: The Unified Payments Interface (UPI) system processed over 10 billion transactions in a single month in 2023.

GS2.headline: India and Maldives Hold High-Level Strategic Dialogue Amidst Regional Shifts
GS2.explainer:
• India and Maldives conducted a high-level strategic dialogue, focusing on strengthening bilateral ties, defense cooperation, and regional security.
• Discussions covered shared maritime security concerns in the Indian Ocean Region and collaborative efforts to counter piracy and illegal fishing.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to maintaining a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing respect for international law.
• The dialogue also touched upon ongoing infrastructure projects in the Maldives supported by India and enhancing people-to-people connect.
• This engagement is crucial for stabilizing relations and ensuring mutual security interests amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics in the Indian Ocean.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Maldives held a high-level strategic dialogue.
Focused on bilateral ties, defense cooperation, regional security.
Discussed maritime security in the Indian Ocean Region.
Reiterated commitment to free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Maldives for India's strategic interests in the Indian Ocean Region.
Analyze the key areas of cooperation between India and Maldives, particularly in defense and maritime security.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in maintaining stable bilateral relations amidst evolving geopolitical influences in the region.
Evaluate India's 'Neighbourhood First' policy in the context of its engagement with island nations like Maldives.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Maldives side by side, symbolizing diplomatic ties.
GS2.trivia: Maldives is an archipelago of 1,192 coral islands grouped into 26 natural atolls in the Indian Ocean.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'National Green Hydrogen Mission 2.0' to Bolster Energy Transition
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approves the second phase of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, focusing on scaling up production and domestic manufacturing.
• The mission aims to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• Key components include incentives for electrolyser manufacturing, developing green hydrogen production hubs, and facilitating demand creation.
• Emphasis on research and development (R&D) in cost-reduction technologies and pilot projects for diverse applications.
• Expected to create significant employment opportunities and attract substantial foreign and domestic investment in the renewable energy sector.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission launched to promote green hydrogen production.
Phase 2.0 focuses on scaling production and manufacturing.
Incentives for electrolyser manufacturing are a key part.
Aims for India to be a global hub for green hydrogen.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate commitments.
Analyze the economic potential of the mission in terms of job creation, investment, and export opportunities.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Examine the role of government policies and R&D in fostering a robust green hydrogen ecosystem.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, constituting roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate, Signals Cautious Stance Amidst Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The central bank's stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic slowdowns and geopolitical tensions as key risks to domestic growth and inflation outlook.
• Projections for Q3 and Q4 FY25 inflation and GDP growth were largely maintained, with a watchful eye on food price volatility.
• The decision indicates a cautious approach, prioritizing price stability amidst persistent external headwinds and evolving domestic demand conditions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
Inflation target is 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
Global slowdown and geopolitical tensions cited as risks.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with supporting economic growth in the current global scenario.
Evaluate the impact of monetary policy decisions on various sectors of the Indian economy, including banking, industry, and consumers.
Examine the interplay between fiscal policy and monetary policy in achieving macroeconomic stability in India.
GS3.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India building facade with currency notes in the foreground.
GS3.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Critical Landing Test for Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD)
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone by successfully conducting a crucial landing experiment for its Reusable Launch Vehicle - Technology Demonstrator (RLV-TD).
• The test involved releasing the RLV-TD from a helicopter at an altitude, followed by its autonomous descent and precise landing on a simulated runway.
• This experiment validates key technologies for an orbital reusable launch vehicle, which aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches.
• The RLV-TD program is a crucial step towards developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system for future Indian space missions.
• Such reusable technology is vital for India to remain competitive in the global space launch market and enhance its space exploration capabilities.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV-TD).
Test involved autonomous landing on a simulated runway.
Aims to reduce space launch costs significantly.
Crucial for developing a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India's space program and its global competitiveness.
Analyze the technical challenges involved in developing and deploying reusable launch systems.
Examine how ISRO's RLV-TD program aligns with India's long-term goals in space exploration and commercial space sector.
Evaluate the economic and strategic advantages of reducing space launch costs through reusable technology.
